facts.subject: Denver Building Code
facts.claim: Denver adopted 2021 IBC as basis for Denver Building Code

facts.subject: Denver Energy Code
facts.claim: 2022 Denver Energy Code (DEC) is the current energy code

facts.subject: Cold Climate Heat Pump
facts.claim: Cold climate heat pumps must have COP ≥ 1.5 at 5°F per 2022 Denver Energy Code Section C406.14

facts.subject: Standard Heat Pump
facts.claim: Standard (non-cold-climate) heat pumps must have COP ≥ 1.75 at 5°F per Denver amendment

facts.subject: Energize Denver
facts.claim: Energize Denver Building Performance Policy requires annual benchmarking for buildings 25,000 SF or larger

facts.subject: Energize Denver Penalty
facts.claim: Energize Denver fine for missing benchmarking deadline is $2,000

facts.subject: Energize Denver EUI Penalty
facts.claim: Energize Denver penalty for missing EUI targets is $0.30 per kBtu in excess

facts.subject: Denver Permitting Office
facts.claim: Denver Permitting Office (DPO) targets 180 days for building permit review

facts.subject: DPO Fee Refund
facts.claim: Denver Permitting Office offers up to $10,000 fee refunds if 180-day target is missed

facts.subject: Historical Permit Times
facts.claim: Denver historical permit review times have ranged from 282-391 days before DPO reforms
facts.source: Denver CPD average plan review times

facts.subject: Climate Zone
facts.claim: Denver is in IECC Climate Zone 5B (cool-dry)

facts.subject: Denver Zoning Code
facts.claim: Denver Zoning Code was adopted in 2010

facts.subject: Zoning District Format
facts.claim: Denver zoning uses Context-District-Height format (e.g., C-MX-5, U-MX-3, E-MX-2)

facts.subject: Fabricated Zone Designations
facts.claim: FABRICATION: C-MU, R-MU, T-MU are NOT real Denver zoning designations
facts.note: If a report cites C-MU, R-MU, or T-MU as Denver districts, it's fabricated. Real designations are C-MX, U-MX, E-MX, S-MX.

facts.subject: Article 3 Misreference
facts.claim: FABRICATION: DZC Article 3 covers Suburban Neighborhood Context, NOT mixed-use
facts.note: If a report cites DZC Article 3 or Article 3-8 as covering mixed-use, it's incorrect.

facts.subject: Denver Green Buildings Ordinance
facts.claim: Denver Green Buildings Ordinance applies to new buildings 25,000 SF or larger

facts.subject: Denver Water System Development Charges
facts.claim: Denver Water System Development Charges (tap fees) were updated January 1, 2026

facts.subject: Water Tap Fee Fabrication
facts.claim: FABRICATION: Denver Water tap fees are NOT $1,940 per unit — real fees are significantly higher
facts.note: If a report cites $1,940/unit as Denver Water tap fee, it's fabricated.

facts.subject: Denver Water Residential Fixed Charge
facts.claim: Denver Water 2026 fixed monthly charge for single-family 3/4-inch meter is $20.91

facts.subject: 2022 DEC Carbon Reduction
facts.claim: 2022 Denver Energy Code reduces operational carbon for commercial new construction by 15% over 2021 IECC

facts.subject: Electric Resistance Limit
facts.claim: 2022 Denver Energy Code limits electric resistance heating to 1.35 W/SF of conditioned area

facts.subject: Solar Ready Requirement
facts.claim: 2022 Denver Energy Code requires solar-ready zone for R1/R2 residential occupancies

facts.subject: Electrical Panel Minimum
facts.claim: 2022 Denver Energy Code requires 200A minimum electrical panel with reserved space for solar/storage

facts.subject: IBC General Adoption
facts.claim: Denver uses the International Building Code as basis for its building code

facts.subject: IECC General Reference
facts.claim: Denver references the International Energy Conservation Code for energy requirements

facts.subject: ADA Standards
facts.claim: Denver enforces ADA 2010 Standards for Accessible Design for accessibility
facts.source: ADA enforcement is federal, applies in Denver

facts.subject: IFC General Reference
facts.claim: Denver Fire Code is based on International Fire Code

facts.subject: IPC General Reference
facts.claim: Denver Plumbing Code is based on International Plumbing Code

facts.subject: Zone 5B
facts.claim: Climate Zone 5B applies to Denver

facts.subject: Seismic Design Category
facts.claim: Denver typically falls in Seismic Design Category B (low seismic risk)
facts.source: IBC seismic maps, Colorado is low seismic risk

facts.subject: CPD
facts.claim: Denver Community Planning and Development (CPD) handles planning, zoning, and building functions

facts.subject: MX Zoning Districts
facts.claim: Denver uses MX (Mixed Use) districts for mixed-use development in Urban (U-MX), Commercial (C-MX), Edge (E-MX), and Suburban (S-MX) contexts

facts.subject: ADA 2010 Standards
facts.claim: ADA 2010 Standards for Accessible Design are the federal accessibility standard

facts.subject: ICC A117.1
facts.claim: ICC A117.1 is the technical standard for accessible design referenced by building codes

facts.subject: Denver Permitting Office
facts.claim: Denver created the Denver Permitting Office (DPO) to streamline permits, with 180-day review target

facts.subject: Affordable Housing Bonus
facts.claim: Denver offers density and height bonuses for affordable housing projects

facts.subject: DZC §13.1.5.3 Fabrication
facts.claim: FABRICATION: DZC §13.1.5.3 is not a verified section for setbacks. Block-sensitive setbacks exist but the section number must come from search results.
facts.source: Section number not verified in Denver Zoning Code
facts.note: Block-sensitive setback is a real concept but this specific section number was fabricated by the LLM.

facts.subject: CADA Wrong Citation
facts.claim: FABRICATION: Colorado Anti-Discrimination Act (CADA) is Title 24 Article 34, NOT Title 9 Article 5
facts.note: Title 9 Article 5 may reference accessibility statutes but calling it CADA is incorrect.

facts.subject: IBC Allowable Area
facts.claim: IBC Table 506.2 provides allowable building areas by occupancy and construction type

facts.subject: IBC Allowable Height
facts.claim: IBC Table 504.3 provides allowable building heights in feet by construction type

facts.subject: IBC Occupancy Separation
facts.claim: IBC Table 508.4 provides required fire separation ratings between occupancy groups

facts.subject: IBC Occupant Load
facts.claim: IBC Table 1004.5 provides occupant load factors by function of space

facts.subject: IBC Sprinkler Requirements
facts.claim: IBC Section 903.2 contains automatic sprinkler system requirements

facts.subject: IBC Fire Resistance
facts.claim: IBC Table 601 provides required fire-resistance ratings by construction type

facts.subject: IBC Fire Separation Distance
facts.claim: IBC Table 602 provides fire-resistance rating requirements based on fire separation distance

facts.subject: IECC Figure C301.1
facts.claim: IECC Figure C301.1 is the climate zone map for commercial buildings

facts.subject: IECC Envelope Requirements
facts.claim: IECC Section C402 contains commercial building envelope requirements (C402.1.3 for assemblies)

facts.subject: NFPA 13 Sprinkler Standard
facts.claim: NFPA 13 is the standard for installation of sprinkler systems in commercial buildings

facts.subject: NFPA 72 Fire Alarm
facts.claim: NFPA 72 is the National Fire Alarm and Signaling Code

facts.subject: ADA Parking Requirements
facts.claim: ADA Section 208 provides accessible parking space requirements

facts.subject: ADA Accessible Routes
facts.claim: ADA Section 206 provides accessible route and entrance requirements

facts.subject: Fair Housing Act Trigger
facts.claim: Fair Housing Act accessibility requirements apply to multifamily buildings with 4 or more units
